Title: Identification and overexpression of RNA-decapping protein GhLSM1BS: Enhancing cotton somatic embryogenesis through up-regulating brassinosteroid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LSM1/Sm-like RNA-binding protein; truncated Sm domain splice isoform
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in roots and stems
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 25-day callus during somatic embryogenesis; transgenic OE lines 8, 12, and 17 show elevated expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth and development; promotes cell proliferation and elongation; enhances somatic embryogenesis/callus proliferation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of GhLSM1BS increases brassinosteroid accumulation (castasterone detectable, typhasterol increased) and enhances callus proliferation during early somatic embryogenesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression during Agrobacterium infection; stable overexpression in cotton; callus proliferation measurements; cytological analysis; RNA-seq; brassinosteroid metabolome analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI SRA PRJNA1240312; BR metabolome analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transformation/infection during somatic embryogenesis induction
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core gene is a splice variant of GhLSM1B. GhLSM1BS, but not GhLSM1BL, promoted callus growth. Mechanism inferred through up-regulation of brassinosteroid biosynthesis-associated CYP450 genes rather than direct enzymatic participation in BR biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhLSM1BS is a nucleus-localized GhLSM1B splice variant predominantly expressed in roots and stems. 2. GhLSM1BS overexpression, but not GhLSM1BL overexpression, promoted larger and more proliferative callus during early cotton somatic embryogenesis. 3. RNA-seq of GhLSM1BS-OE callus showed enrichment of DEGs in the brassinosteroid biosynthesis pathway, including highly expressed CYP450 genes. 4. GhLSM1BS-OE callus accumulated more castasterone and typhasterol, linking GhLSM1BS to enhanced BR biosynthesis and improved SE.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving cotton transformation and regeneration efficiency by enhancing early somatic embryogenesis. Can be deployed as an engineering target to accelerate molecular breeding, but likely should be used in tissue culture/regeneration contexts because whole-plant pleiotropic effects were not evaluated.
